driver_weekend_notes: Racing Bulls punchy; Lawson scored final sprint point from P9 to P8.
Racing Bulls SQ3 and P10 sprint is a positive midfield signal.
Slight negative after slipping in sprint.
Haas chatter negative despite a small sprint gain.
Williams slight step but still outside top ten; sprint loss.
Williams straight-line/setup concerns and sprint loss.
Slight positive after gaining positions in sprint.
Dropped to last in sprint classification; negative current-weekend signal.
Missed Q3 by only 0.038s and continued a strong recent run for Racing Bulls.
Rapid Q2 lap and P9 qualifying added a positive driver-specific signal for Audi.
Reached Q3 and qualified P8, sustaining Racing Bulls' positive recent midfield signal.
Qualified P12 with a mildly positive midfield signal and no unresolved issue reported.
Qualified P13 with a mildly positive midfield weekend and no unresolved issue reported.
A sudden hydraulic leak ended qualifying in P14 and remains a material pre-race reliability concern.
P15 qualifying left Williams outside the points-contending group despite its Spa updates.
Q1 exit and P16 qualifying were a mild negative, with no separate unresolved mechanical problem reported.
P19 qualifying was slow, but Bottas reported a straightforward weekend, improved confidence and that the team extracted the available package.

Missed Q2 by seven milliseconds and qualified P17, extending Williams' weak Spa weekend signal.
Cadillac's post-qualifying report says an energy-deployment issue present since FP1 was not fixed before qualifying and cost Perez time on Spa's straights.
Spa deployment issue hurt pace, but Lawson receives the full, strongly rated Racing Bulls upgrade package in Hungary.

Back-to-back P8 finishes and competitive race pace establish a strong current midfield signal.
Fifth consecutive points finish and strong upgrade performance suit the lower-straight Hungary layout.
Belgium P10 adds driver momentum, but Alpine says Hungary will not produce a competitive-order turnaround.
Competitive race pace was masked by starting position and weak straight-line speed; Audi's broader trend is positive.
Spa result was compromised, but Bearman reported a more connected car and stronger underlying pace.
Williams' midfield gap is increasing and Sainz says upcoming upgrades will not completely close it.
First-lap damage obscured the race, but Ocon still reported weak straight-line speed, rear grip, and overall pace.
